body.purpose: Sustained prose, editable text, comments, and source code.

ui.purpose: Controls, filenames, compact helper copy, and navigation.

label.purpose: Paths, state, counts, shortcuts, and metadata.

shadows.name: overlay
shadows.purpose: Drawers, review overlays, and the floating line-selection toolbar.

shadows.name: segmented-selection
shadows.purpose: Minute separation for the active option inside a segmented control.

motion.name: reduced-motion
motion.purpose: Effective removal of transitions and animation when reduced motion is requested.

components.name: Primary Snapshot Action
components.description: The largest commitment control, reserved for approving the complete visible snapshot.
components.css: .ds-snapshot-button { display:grid; grid-template-columns:auto minmax(0,1fr); align-items:center; gap:.65rem; width:100%; min-height:3.5rem; padding:.55rem .7rem; border:0; border-radius:.5rem; background:var(--accent); color:var(--on-accent); font:inherit; text-align:left; cursor:pointer; } .ds-snapshot-button:hover { background:var(--accent-strong); } .ds-snapshot-button:focus-visible { outline:2px solid var(--focus); outline-offset:2px; } .ds-snapshot-button:active { transform:translateY(1px); } .ds-snapshot-button span:nth-child(2) { display:grid; gap:.08rem; } .ds-snapshot-button strong { font-size:.875rem; } .ds-snapshot-button small { color:oklch(.92 .018 25); font-size:.75rem; } .ds-snapshot-mark { display:grid; place-items:center; width:1.65rem; height:1.65rem; border-radius:50%; background:oklch(.67 .14 25); } .ds-snapshot-mark svg { width:1rem; fill:none; stroke:currentColor; stroke-linecap:round; stroke-linejoin:round; stroke-width:1.5; }

components.name: Secondary Review Action
components.description: A compact bordered action for approving one file or toggling a supporting panel.
components.css: .ds-review-action { min-height:1.75rem; padding:0 .6rem; border:1px solid var(--line-strong); border-radius:.4rem; background:var(--paper); color:var(--ink-soft); font:600 .875rem/1.4 Inter,ui-sans-serif,sans-serif; cursor:pointer; } .ds-review-action:hover { border-color:var(--success); color:var(--success); } .ds-review-action:focus-visible { outline:2px solid var(--focus); outline-offset:2px; } .ds-review-action:active { transform:translateY(1px); } .ds-review-action:disabled { cursor:not-allowed; opacity:.5; }

components.name: Compact Search Field
components.description: A dark embedded search field for files or the active diff.
components.html: <label class="ds-search"><svg aria-hidden="true" viewBox="0 0 20 20"><circle cx="8.5" cy="8.5" r="5"/><path d="m12.3 12.3 4 4"/></svg><input placeholder="Find in diff" /></label>
components.css: .ds-search { display:flex; align-items:center; gap:.5rem; min-height:2.25rem; padding:0 .7rem; border:1px solid var(--line); border-radius:.42rem; background:var(--canvas); color:var(--ink-muted); } .ds-search:focus-within { outline:2px solid var(--focus); outline-offset:2px; } .ds-search svg { width:1rem; fill:none; stroke:currentColor; stroke-linecap:round; stroke-width:1.5; } .ds-search input { min-width:0; width:100%; border:0; outline:0; background:transparent; color:var(--ink); font:400 .875rem/1.4 Inter,ui-sans-serif,sans-serif; } .ds-search input::placeholder { color:var(--ink-muted); }

components.name: Review Status Capsule
components.description: An explicit semantic label for snapshot and file state.
components.css: .ds-status { display:inline-block; padding:.25rem .45rem; border-radius:999px; font:700 .75rem/1.4 SFMono-Regular,Cascadia Code,monospace; letter-spacing:.04em; text-transform:uppercase; } .ds-status-current { background:var(--success-soft); color:var(--success); } .ds-status-changed { margin-left:.4rem; background:var(--warning-soft); color:var(--warning); }

components.name: Active File Row
components.description: The selected queue item, combining file identity, review state, and compact metadata.
components.css: .ds-file-row { display:grid; grid-template-columns:1rem minmax(0,1fr) auto; align-items:center; gap:.55rem; width:100%; min-height:2.45rem; padding:.3rem .4rem; border:1px solid var(--line); border-radius:.42rem; background:var(--paper-deep); color:var(--ink-soft); text-align:left; cursor:pointer; } .ds-file-row:hover { background:var(--paper-hover); } .ds-file-row:focus-visible { outline:2px solid var(--focus); outline-offset:2px; } .ds-file-mark { color:var(--accent); font:700 1rem/1 SFMono-Regular,Cascadia Code,monospace; } .ds-file-copy { display:grid; min-width:0; gap:.06rem; } .ds-file-copy strong { overflow:hidden; font:600 .875rem/1.4 Inter,ui-sans-serif,sans-serif; text-overflow:ellipsis; white-space:nowrap; } .ds-file-copy small { color:var(--ink-muted); font-size:.75rem; } .ds-file-meta { color:var(--ink-muted); font:400 .75rem/1.4 SFMono-Regular,Cascadia Code,monospace; }

components.name: Visible Queue Approval
components.description: Atomic approval for two or more reviewable files in the current filtered queue.
components.css: .ds-visible-approval { display:grid; grid-template-columns:auto minmax(0,1fr); align-items:center; gap:.55rem; width:100%; min-height:2.75rem; padding:.45rem .55rem; border:1px solid var(--line-strong); border-radius:.42rem; background:var(--paper-deep); color:var(--ink-soft); font-family:Inter,ui-sans-serif,sans-serif; text-align:left; cursor:pointer; } .ds-visible-approval:hover { border-color:var(--success); background:var(--success-soft); color:var(--success); } .ds-visible-approval:focus-visible { outline:2px solid var(--focus); outline-offset:2px; } .ds-visible-approval svg { width:1rem; fill:none; stroke:currentColor; stroke-linecap:round; stroke-linejoin:round; stroke-width:1.5; } .ds-visible-approval span { display:grid; gap:.08rem; } .ds-visible-approval strong { font-size:.875rem; font-weight:600; } .ds-visible-approval small { color:var(--ink-muted); font-size:.75rem; line-height:1.4; }

components.name: Vim Mode Guide
components.description: A visible modal-state strip for cursor-driven Vim navigation with a hidden browser scrollbar and six-line scroll margin.
components.html: Visual lineJK moveV finish selectionC commentA approveEsc exit
components.css: .ds-vim-guide { display:flex; align-items:center; flex-wrap:wrap; gap:.45rem .9rem; min-height:2.15rem; padding:.35rem 1rem; border-bottom:1px solid var(--line); background:var(--paper-deep); color:var(--ink-muted); font:400 .75rem/1.4 Inter,ui-sans-serif,sans-serif; } .ds-vim-guide strong { color:var(--accent-strong); font-family:SFMono-Regular,Cascadia Code,monospace; text-transform:uppercase; } .ds-vim-guide span { display:inline-flex; align-items:center; gap:.22rem; }

components.name: Comment Record
components.description: A local evidence record with exact line metadata and restrained destructive action.
components.html:
New lines 42 to 45Just now

Handle the empty workspace before rendering this branch.
components.css: .ds-comment { padding:.65rem; border:1px solid var(--line); border-radius:.42rem; background:var(--canvas); color:var(--ink-soft); } .ds-comment header { display:flex; justify-content:space-between; gap:.75rem; color:var(--ink-muted); font:400 .75rem/1.4 SFMono-Regular,Cascadia Code,monospace; } .ds-comment p { margin:.45rem 0; color:var(--ink-soft); font:400 1rem/1.6 Inter,ui-sans-serif,sans-serif; } .ds-comment button { padding:0; border:0; background:transparent; color:var(--ink-muted); font:400 .75rem/1.4 Inter,ui-sans-serif,sans-serif; text-decoration:underline; cursor:pointer; } .ds-comment button:hover { color:var(--ink); } .ds-comment button:focus-visible { outline:2px solid var(--focus); outline-offset:2px; }

components.name: Selected Diff Line
components.description: The signature source row, with secondary line numbers and a restrained selected field.
components.css: .ds-diff-line { display:grid; grid-template-columns:2.8rem 1rem minmax(0,1fr); align-items:stretch; min-height:1.625rem; background:var(--accent-soft); color:var(--ink-soft); font:400 1rem/1.6 SFMono-Regular,Cascadia Code,Liberation Mono,monospace; } .ds-diff-line button { border:0; border-right:1px solid var(--line); background:transparent; color:var(--ink-muted); font:inherit; cursor:pointer; } .ds-diff-line button:hover { color:var(--ink); } .ds-diff-line button:focus-visible { outline:2px solid var(--focus); outline-offset:-2px; } .ds-diff-line > span { color:var(--accent-strong); text-align:center; } .ds-diff-line code { padding:0 .5rem; color:var(--syntax-text); white-space:pre; } .ds-diff-line b { color:var(--syntax-keyword); font-weight:inherit; } .ds-diff-line i { color:var(--syntax-constant); font-style:normal; }

components.name: First Review Guidance
components.description: Dismissible inline teaching for line selection, keyboard movement, and narrow-screen panning.
components.html: Select line numbers to leave a note.Use J and K to move between files, or / to search.
components.css: .ds-review-hint { display:grid; grid-template-columns:auto minmax(0,1fr) auto; align-items:start; gap:.75rem; padding:.7rem 1rem; border-bottom:1px solid var(--line); background:var(--accent-quiet); color:var(--ink-soft); font:400 .875rem/1.4 Inter,ui-sans-serif,sans-serif; } .ds-review-hint svg { width:1rem; fill:none; stroke:var(--accent-strong); stroke-linecap:round; stroke-linejoin:round; stroke-width:1.5; } .ds-review-hint span { display:grid; gap:.12rem; } .ds-review-hint strong { color:var(--ink); } .ds-review-hint button { min-height:2rem; padding:0 .6rem; border:1px solid var(--line-strong); border-radius:.35rem; background:transparent; color:var(--ink-soft); font:600 .75rem/1.4 Inter,ui-sans-serif,sans-serif; cursor:pointer; } .ds-review-hint button:hover { background:var(--paper-deep); color:var(--ink); } .ds-review-hint button:focus-visible { outline:2px solid var(--focus); outline-offset:2px; }

components.name: Undo Notice
components.description: Focused, time-bounded recovery after removing review evidence.
components.css: .ds-undo { display:flex; align-items:center; gap:1rem; max-width:32rem; padding:.7rem .75rem; border:1px solid var(--line-strong); border-radius:.5rem; background:var(--paper-deep); color:var(--ink-soft); font:400 .875rem/1.4 Inter,ui-sans-serif,sans-serif; box-shadow:0 8px 24px oklch(.08 .008 270 / .34); } .ds-undo span { min-width:0; overflow-wrap:anywhere; } .ds-undo button { min-height:2rem; padding:0 .65rem; border:0; border-radius:.35rem; background:var(--ink); color:var(--paper); font:600 .875rem/1.4 Inter,ui-sans-serif,sans-serif; cursor:pointer; } .ds-undo button:focus-visible { outline:2px solid var(--focus); outline-offset:2px; }

narrative.overview:
Redline feels like a dedicated proofing desk used by a developer during a long review session in a dim room. The dark theme is a deliberate low-eye-strain environment, not a stylistic shortcut. Cool graphite surfaces hold steady while restrained red-pencil marks identify the few moments that require action.

The system is precise, calm, and deliberate. Dense information is welcome when it supports inspection, but every region has one job and every state has an explicit label. The interface earns trust through stable rails, exact alignment, quiet borders, and controls that behave like familiar instruments.

It rejects the visual language of a generic SaaS dashboard, a cheerful collaboration product, and a full IDE. It also rejects decorative cards, workflow sprawl, playful language, and controls unrelated to inspection.
keyCharacteristics: Dark graphite surfaces tuned for sustained low-light reading.
One red-pencil accent reserved for selection, attention, and primary commitment.
Compact, keyboard-first controls with visible focus and explicit state labels.
Structural rails and dividers instead of floating card grids.
Monospaced metadata for paths, counts, line numbers, and machine state.
Responsive structure that turns rails into focus-managed overlays below their desktop thresholds.

rules.name: The Fixed Desk Rule
rules.body: The diff is the work surface. Navigation and review history support it without competing for visual priority.

rules.name: The Low-Strain Rule
rules.body: Large areas remain low-chroma and low-contrast. High chroma is rare, semantic, and never used as decoration.

rules.name: The Red Pencil Rule
rules.body: Red Pencil occupies no more than ten percent of a screen. Its rarity is what makes it authoritative.

rules.name: The State Has a Name Rule
rules.body: Approval, changed, error, and warning states always include text or an icon. Color alone never carries meaning.

rules.name: The Quiet Code Rule
rules.body: Diff backgrounds carry additions and removals; syntax color remains subordinate to the change itself.

rules.name: The Evidence First Rule
rules.body: Source code is the largest sustained text on the review screen. Interface chrome never grows loud enough to challenge it.

rules.name: The Mono Means Exact Rule
rules.body: Use monospace for paths, counts, line numbers, fingerprints, shortcuts, timestamps, and state metadata. Do not use it as atmosphere.

rules.name: The Flat Desk Rule
rules.body: Surfaces at rest are flat. If a stationary desktop region needs a shadow to separate it, its tonal layer or divider is wrong.

rules.name: The Overlay Earns Lift Rule
rules.body: Shadows are permitted only when a surface physically covers another surface or floats above the diff.

dos: Do preserve the dark theme and cool graphite surface ladder for low eye strain during sustained review.
Do keep sustained prose and source code at 1rem, controls at 0.875rem, and secondary labels no smaller than 0.75rem.
Do keep Red Pencil rare and attach it to selection, attention, or commitment.
Do use one-pixel rules and tonal layers to define structure.
Do keep the diff visually primary and source code more prominent than surrounding chrome.
Do label every semantic state with copy or iconography in addition to color.
Do preserve visible focus, keyboard navigation, focus-managed overlays, reduced-motion behavior, and 2.75rem coarse-pointer targets.
Do use familiar controls with consistent hover, focus, active, disabled, loading, and error behavior.
Do keep first-use teaching contextual, dismissible, and replayable through persistent shortcut help.
Do provide inline undo for destructive review-history actions.
donts: Don't make Redline resemble a generic SaaS dashboard.
Don't make it feel like a cheerful collaboration product or a full IDE.
Don't add decorative cards, identical card grids, nested cards, or a card wrapper around every region.
Don't introduce workflow sprawl, playful language, or controls unrelated to inspection.
Don't use gradients, gradient text, glassmorphism, neon accents, or saturated inactive states.
Don't use a colored side stripe thicker than one pixel on records, alerts, or list items.
Don't invent display typography for product chrome or use monospace merely to look technical.
Don't communicate review status through color alone.
Don't add decorative motion. Motion exists only to explain state, loading, or overlay movement.
Don't reach for a modal first. Prefer stable rails, inline disclosure, or the established focus-managed drawers.
